snippets.title: Shannon entropy from a PMF (NumPy)
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

# Fair die: values 1..6, PMF uniform
p = np.ones(6) / 6

# Use base-2 log for bits; mask p=0 to avoid log(0)
def shannon_entropy(probs, base=2):
    p = np.asarray(probs, dtype=float)
    p = p[p > 0]
    logs = np.log(p) / np.log(base)
    return -np.sum(p * logs)

H_bits = shannon_entropy(p, base=2)
print(f"H(X) = {H_bits:.4f} bits")  # log2(6) ≈ 2.585
snippets.explanation: Sum only over outcomes with p > 0; 0 · log 0 is treated as 0 by convention. Result is in bits when base=2.

snippets.title: Binary entropy for Bernoulli(p)
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

def binary_entropy(p):
    p = np.clip(p, 1e-12, 1 - 1e-12)
    return -(p * np.log2(p) + (1 - p) * np.log2(1 - p))

p_grid = np.linspace(0.001, 0.999, 200)
H = binary_entropy(p_grid)

print(f"Max at p=0.5: {H.max():.4f} bits")  # 1.0
print(f"At p=0.9: {binary_entropy(0.9):.4f} bits")
snippets.explanation: Binary entropy peaks at p = 0.5 (1 bit) and approaches 0 as p → 0 or p → 1. Define shannon_entropy from the previous snippet or inline the same formula.

snippets.title: Entropy and information gain with scikit-learn
snippets.code:
from sklearn.metrics import entropy
import numpy as np

# Class counts at a node: 50 stay, 50 leave
root_counts = np.array([50, 50])
H_root = entropy(root_counts, base=2)

# After split: left pure [40, 0], right pure [0, 10]
H_left = entropy(np.array([40, 0]), base=2)
H_right = entropy(np.array([0, 10]), base=2)

# Weighted child entropy (40 + 10 = 50 samples in children)
H_children = (40/50) * H_left + (10/50) * H_right
info_gain = H_root - H_children

print(f"H(root) = {H_root:.4f}, info gain = {info_gain:.4f} bits")
snippets.explanation: `sklearn.metrics.entropy` accepts count vectors and returns Shannon entropy in the chosen log base. Information gain = parent H − weighted child H.

snippets.title: Label distribution entropy in classification
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from collections import Counter

# Imbalanced labels in a training set
labels = ["cat"] * 900 + ["dog"] * 90 + ["bird"] * 10
counts = np.array(list(Counter(labels).values()), dtype=float)
probs = counts / counts.sum()

H_labels = -np.sum(probs * np.log2(probs))
print(f"Label entropy: {H_labels:.3f} bits")
print(f"Max for 3 classes: {np.log2(3):.3f} bits")
snippets.explanation: Entropy of the label distribution measures how balanced classes are. Highly skewed data yields lower H than a uniform three-class split.
